authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's CCBHC criteria directly govern first-contact triage, timely evaluation, screening, risk assessment, inability-to-pay handling, and service-access posture for community behavioral health settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: First-contact triage, intake access, and prerequisite review
used_for: required intake facts such as referral source, reason for care, and releases
access and no-denial posture before downstream service routing

authoritative_sources.title: Community Mental Health Centers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Conditions of Participation guidance is authoritative for community mental health center admission appropriateness, client-rights obligations, and the boundary between intake handling and interdisciplinary treatment-team ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Screening, Brief Intervention, and Referral to Treatment (SBIRT)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA SBIRT guidance is the public workflow reference for substance-use screening, brief intervention, and referral sequencing, which materially shapes intake terminology and warm-handoff routing for behavioral health access.

authoritative_sources.title: National Behavioral Health Crisis Care Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's national crisis guidance provides the behavioral-health-specific framework for when routine intake must stop and route to crisis assets such as 988, mobile crisis, and stabilization services.

authoritative_sources.title: Information Related to Mental and Behavioral Health, including Opioid Overdose
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for HIPAA treatment, family-sharing, personal-representative, and health-and-safety disclosure rules as applied to mental health and substance use disorder information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Consent, disclosure, and minor or representative review

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 2 -- Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder Patient Records
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 42 CFR Part 2 is the controlling federal confidentiality rule for covered substance use disorder diagnosis, treatment, and referral records and materially changes disclosure and consent behavior during intake and routing.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior-authorization interoperability guidance is an authoritative public control baseline for how payer authorization dependencies should be surfaced, exchanged, and treated in health access workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and behavioral-health context normalization
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the request is behavioral-health or community-care intake work and normalize presenting need, referral source, identity, contact channel, and program context into a structured intake shape.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Consent, privacy, and disclosure gate review
workflow_stages.description: Check releases of information, Part 2 coverage, guardian or personal-representative status, and disclosure permissions before routing records or discussing treatment details.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, referral, and authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm payer or program eligibility, required referrals, prior authorization status, catchment or program-enrollment fit, and reservation or scheduling dependencies before work moves downstream.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Immediate-safety and licensed-authority triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify acute risk, crisis indicators, or facts that require crisis-response or licensed-clinical ownership instead of routine intake continuation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Acceptance, pend, rejection, and routing package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Create a traceable disposition note, prerequisite checklist, or escalation packet that records what is satisfied, missing, or routed elsewhere.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source conflict and jurisdiction escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when payer rules, tenant policy, minor-consent law, or local crisis procedures materially change the correct intake outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Referral, self-referral, or discharge-follow-up intake packet
Verified identity, demographic, and contact records
Release-of-information, Part 2 consent, and personal-representative documents
Documented screening or triage outputs and referral source notes
Payer eligibility, referral, prior authorization, and benefit-verification records
Program enrollment, catchment, reservation, waitlist, and scheduling-hold records
EHR, practice-management, and crisis-routing disposition records
systems_of_record: Behavioral health EHR or clinical record system
Practice management, patient access, and scheduling platform
Payer eligibility and prior-authorization portals or clearinghouse tools
Consent and privacy document management system
Crisis documentation, call-center, or 988 or mobile-crisis handoff log

allowed: Validate intake completeness, identity, consent, referral, eligibility, authorization, and access readiness before downstream work starts
Accept, reject, pend, or route cases based on documented prerequisites and ownership boundaries
Prepare evidence-backed disposition notes, checklists, and escalation packets without overstating authority
Normalize behavioral-health terminology such as ROI, SBIRT, Part 2, warm handoff, and crisis-routing triggers into bounded intake outputs
escalate: Acute safety, suicide, homicide, overdose, withdrawal, or psychosis facts that require crisis or licensed-clinical review
Part 2, HIPAA, minor-consent, or personal-representative ambiguity that changes what may be disclosed or routed
Conflicting payer, tenant, jurisdiction, or program rules regarding referral, prior authorization, or catchment access
Missing tenant system-of-record context for program capacity, reservation, or scheduling ownership
refuse: Request to diagnose, determine level of care, or provide crisis counseling
Request to approve or deny treatment, admission, or medical necessity
Request to disclose protected behavioral health or substance use disorder records without valid authority
Request to bypass referral, authorization, or safety prerequisites just to move intake forward

domain_failure_modes: Routine intake proceeds even though crisis facts require immediate safety routing.
Substance use disorder records are disclosed or redisclosed without valid Part 2 authority.
The lane behaves like generic scheduling or customer support and ignores referral, payer, consent, and EHR dependencies.
Minor-consent, parent-access, or personal-representative rules are guessed instead of escalated when jurisdiction changes the answer.
Program-capacity, catchment, or reservation blockers are ignored and downstream work starts without a valid access path.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State minor-consent and parental-access rules for mental health and substance use treatment
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May materially change whether records can be shared with a parent or whether the minor alone controls disclosure and reimbursement consent.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant or jurisdiction-specific policy retrieval and escalate instead of assuming default parent access.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Plan-specific referral and prior-authorization requirements by product, program, and locality
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change whether intake can be accepted, pended, or routed before scheduling or treatment start.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use payer portal and tenant utilization-management retrieval as the controlling source and treat public guidance as baseline only.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: County catchment, walk-in eligibility, and local crisis-routing conventions for community behavioral health programs
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May alter which program is responsible for access, same-day evaluation, or crisis diversion at first contact.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve local program and crisis-routing policy from tenant systems and escalate when unavailable.

notes: The lane is materially different from generic intake because acute safety triggers, Part 2 confidentiality, behavioral-health-specific referrals, and payer-program access dependencies change both what can be accepted and what can be disclosed.
Public sources define the intake and triage baseline, but payer, jurisdiction, and local-program rules remain tenant retrieval dependencies that must not be guessed.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated status because unresolved policy variants are converted into explicit escalation outputs rather than silent assumptions.
